sections.paragraph:
δεσμεύω 1 aor. 3 sg. ἐδέσμευσε (TestSol); subj. 2 sg. (Sir 12:12 Bcorr). Pass.: impf. ἐδεσμευόμην; aor. inf. δεσμευθῆναι (TestSol 13:7)

① bind (Eur.; X.; Pla., Leg. 7, 808d; Epict. 4, 1, 127; PGM 4, 1246:5, 320; Judg 16:11 B; cp. 3 Macc 5:5; TestSol; ParJer 7:26 [ABH]; Jos., Ant. 14, 348) τινά Lk 8:29; Ac 22:4; τὴν πᾶσαν σάρκα ἀνθρώπων πρὸς ἡδονὴν ἐδέσμευεν (the devil) bound all flesh of humans to pleasure AcPlCor 2:11.

② tie up (in a bundle) (Hes., Op. 481 grain in a sheaf; Polyb.; PHib 214, 1 [c. 250 b.c.]; Sb 9386, 4; 9409 [1], 76 and 77; PLond I, 131 recto, 426 and 437 p. 182f; PFlor 322, 31; Gen 37:7; Jdth 8:3; Am 2:8) in imagery φορτίον tie up a burden, load which is then put on a person’s shoulder Mt 23:4 (cp. Cicero, Tusc. Disp. II, 4, 11).—DELG s.v. δέω 1. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. sg. = singular

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

impf. impf. = imperfect

inf. inf. = infinitive

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

ParJer ParJer = Paraleipomena Jeremiou, II? a.d.—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

PHib PHib = The Hibeh Papyri I–II—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

PFlor PFlor = Papiri Fiorentini—List 4

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 219
